name: Rakuten Advertising Affiliate API
what_it_does: Rakuten Advertising REST API for affiliate program management, publisher network integration, and performance marketing automation. Enables AI agents to manage affiliate publisher recruitment and program onboarding for affiliate network scaling, handle commission tracking and automated payment calculation for publisher compensation, access click and conversion reporting for affiliate program performance analytics, retrieve publisher performance data and activity reporting for affiliate management, manage creative asset and deep link generation for affiliate marketing materials, handle fraud detection and coupon affiliate management for affiliate quality control, access omnichannel attribution including in-app and mobile for cross-platform affiliate tracking, retrieve real-time reporting APIs for affiliate analytics automation, manage affiliate program configuration and commission rule management for program optimization, and integrate Rakuten Advertising data with analytics, DMP, and financial platforms.
use_cases: Managing affiliate publisher programs from marketing agents
Tracking commission performance from affiliate analytics agents
Recruiting new publishers from partner development agents
Reporting affiliate ROI from performance marketing agents
not_for: Small businesses without dedicated affiliate program management
Direct-to-consumer brands without affiliate channel revenue
best_when: A brand or retailer using Rakuten Advertising (formerly LinkShare) wants AI agents to automate affiliate publisher management, commission tracking, creative distribution, and affiliate program performance reporting.
avoid_when: FTC DISCLOSURE RISK: Automated affiliate content or promotion must include required FTC material connection disclosures (#ad, #sponsored, #affiliate); automated affiliate content without disclosure creates brand liability and FTC enforcement risk. Publisher fraud — coupon and deal affiliate fraud (unauthorized coupon codes, browser extension hijacking, last-click attribution manipulation) is prevalent in affiliate networks; automated commission approval without fraud validation creates margin erosion. Commission fraud in financial affiliate — financial product affiliates (credit cards, insurance) have high lead fraud rates; automated lead approval creates acquisition cost overruns.

auth.notes: Rakuten Advertising uses OAuth 2.0. Developer portal at developer.rakutenadvertising.com. REST API with JSON. No native webhooks (polling-based). New York, New York HQ (Rakuten Advertising). Rakuten Group (Tokyo, Japan; Tokyo Stock Exchange). Rakuten Advertising formerly LinkShare (acquired 2005). 150,000+ publishers globally. $1B+ in managed commissions. Major retail and travel affiliate network. Competes with Impact.com, CJ Affiliate, and Partnerize for affiliate network.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — annual network fee and percentage of managed spend
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual network access fee; percentage of commissions managed
pricing.notes: New York, NY. Rakuten Group (Tokyo). LinkShare acquired 2005. 150,000+ publishers. $1B+ managed commissions. Major retail/travel affiliate network. Competes with Impact.com and CJ for affiliate.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; documented in developer portal

known_agent_gotchas: FTC DISCLOSURE REQUIREMENT: Automated affiliate or publisher content amplification must include FTC material connection disclosure; EU/UK programs require ASA/CAP Code equivalent; automated content without disclosure creates brand enforcement risk
No webhooks — affiliate conversion event notification requires polling; no native push for commission events, making real-time commission tracking require frequent API polling

Coupon affiliate fraud — Rakuten Advertising has large coupon and deal publisher segment; automated commission approval must detect unauthorized coupon codes, browser extension hijacking, and last-click attribution fraud

Rakuten Group data sharing — Rakuten Advertising data flows within broader Rakuten ecosystem (Rakuten.com marketplace, Rakuten Bank, etc.); understand cross-Rakuten data sharing implications for publisher and advertiser data
GDPR tracking consent — Rakuten Advertising tracking for EU traffic requires cookie consent compliance; automated EU affiliate tracking without consent verification violates GDPR Article 6
API reporting latency — Rakuten Advertising reports have 24-48 hour data lag in some report types; automated bidding or optimization based on Rakuten data must account for reporting latency
agent_readiness.security_notes: Affiliate marketing network. SOC2. OAuth2. US/EU. Publisher and affiliate commission data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Affiliate network. SOC2. OAuth2. US/EU. Publisher and commission data.
